rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Carrier Corporation¿s demurrer to the first, second, third, fourth, sixth and seventh causes of action in plaintiffs¿ second amended complaint is overruled. Plaintiffs¿ causes of action contain allegations sufficient to state the bases for their claims against defendant. This includes sufficient product identification under Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71, and sufficient allegations as to the false representation (Restatement 402-B) and intentional tort (Civil Code §§ 1708, 1709 and 1710) causes of action. Defendant has 10 days to answer. The hearing on this demurrer will be at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. = (220/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JIM KELLY et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs' Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Carrier Corporation¿s motion to strike plaintiffs¿ prayer for punitive damages is granted with 10 days leave to amend. The court did not consider plaintiffs¿ opposition. Pursuant to C.C.P. § 1005(b), plaintiffs¿ opposition was due by August 11. Plaintiffs did not file their opposition until August 19, only 3 court days before this hearing. Plaintiffs¿ opposition and attorney declaration failed to demonstrate any good cause for the court to consider the late-filed opposition. See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019). The hearing on this motion will be at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. = (220/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LOWELL R. HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Groeniger & Company¿s demurrer to plaintiffs¿ complaint is continued to September 7 to be heard at the same time as its motion to strike. If there is notice that any party contests this tentative ruling, the hearing will be on August 24 at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. = (220/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: LOWELL R. HOELMER et al VS. 3M COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 24, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Groeniger & Company¿s motion to strike is continued to September 7 to allow defendant time to file a reply. Plaintiffs filed a late opposition that included an attorney declaration demonstrating good cause for the court to consider the opposition. The court continues the hearing to give defendant time to reply. See Hobson v. Raychem Corp. (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 614 (disapproved on other grounds in Colmenares v. Braemar Country Club, Inc. (2003) 29 Cal.4th 1019). Defendant¿s reply, if any, must be filed and served by August 30. If there is notice that any party contests this tentative ruling, the hearing will be on August 24 at 9:30am in Dept. 503, Hon. Teri L. Jackson presiding. = (220/TLJ)
